revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

"plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia21]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

revision.comment: 誤用訂正。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大崎八幡宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Osakimachimangu.JPG|270px]]
拝殿（国宝） |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]八幡四丁目6番1号 |緯度度 = 38 |緯度分 = 16 |緯度秒 = 21.1 |経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 50 |経度秒 = 42 |ISO = JP-04 |祭神 = [[応神天皇]]、[[仲哀天皇]]、[[神功皇后]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月14日]] - [[9月17日|17日]] |神事 = 松焚祭（[[どんと祭]]） |地図 = Sendai#Japan Miyagi }} {{座標一覧}} '''大崎八幡宮'''（おおさきはちまんぐう）は、[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]八幡にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。社殿（本殿・石の間・拝殿）は[[国宝]]に指定されており、[[どんと祭]]の裸参りで知られる。 == 祭神 == * [[応神天皇]]、[[仲哀天皇]]、[[神功皇后]] * [[江戸時代]]に仙台で盛んとなった卦体神（けたいがみ、[[十二支]]の[[守り本尊]]）信仰に伴い、[[乾]]の守り本尊である[[阿弥陀如来]]とされたことから、現在でも戌亥歳生まれの人からの崇敬を集めている。 == 社名・歴史== [[ファイル:NDL-DC 762376 0208 0023crd-OsakiHachimanJinja.jpg|thumb|240px|right|[[1912年]]（[[明治]]45年）頃の大崎八幡神社]] 創建年代は不明であるが、社伝では[[坂上田村麻呂]]が[[宇佐神宮]]を[[鎮守府 (古代)|鎮守府]][[胆沢城]]（現[[岩手県]][[奥州市]]水沢）に[[勧請]]し'''[[鎮守府八幡宮]]'''と称したことに始まり、[[室町時代]]に入り[[奥州管領]]であった[[大崎氏]]が自領地内（現[[宮城県]][[大崎市]][[田尻町 (宮城県)|田尻町]]）に遷したため、大崎八幡宮と呼ばれるようになったという。 大崎氏改易後の[[慶長]]9年（[[1604年]]）、[[伊達政宗]]が[[仙台城]]（{{Coord|38|15|11.2|N|140|51|23.3|E|region:JP-04|name=仙台城（本丸）}}）から見て北西（[[乾]]）かつ仙台城の[[城下町]]（{{Coord|38|15|36.9|N|140|52|14.5|E|region:JP-04|name=仙台城下町の中心地「芭蕉の辻」}}）北西端の現在地に造営を始めた。当地は[[別当寺]]の[[龍寶寺 (仙台市)|龍寶寺]]（{{Coord|38|16|17.4|N|140|50|45|E|region:JP-04|name=龍宝寺（別当寺）}}）の西隣にあり、城下町の北に連なる北山丘陵（[[七北田丘陵]]）と[[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]とが接するために城下町がある[[河岸段丘]]の西端部にあたり、広瀬川上流の[[愛子盆地]]や[[山形県|山形]]方面に向かう[[作並街道]]（現[[国道48号]]）が超えなくてはいけない最初の難所の鶏沢（{{Coord|38|16|12.2|N|140|50|35.5|E|region:JP-04|name=一部暗渠化された鶏沢に架かる鶏橋}}）[http://www.hirosegawa-net.com/100sen/naka_001.php 鶏橋・鶏沢]（仙台市「広瀬川ホームページ」） の城下町側に接する地である[http://eichi.library.pref.miyagi.jp/ezu/ 古絵地図]（[[宮城県図書館]]）[http://www.udworks.net/katteren/katte_site/hachiman/_adventure/pict/2m1786_1.jpg 仙台城下絵図/天明6～寛政元年(1786～89)]（勝手連 仙臺まちづくり応援団「[http://www.udworks.net/katteren/katte_site/hachiman/index.html ゲート・タウン八幡]」）。[[刑部左衛門国次]]([[左甚五郎]]のモデルの一人と言われる)という[[彫刻]]職人が建立に携わった。 [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）、従来[[伊達氏]]が祀ってきた[[成島八幡神社|成島八幡宮]]と合祀して[[遷座]]し、仙台城下の乾（戌亥・北西）[[天門 (方位)|天門]]の鎮めとした（当宮の西隣の鶏沢には堤も造られた）。以前に大崎氏の家臣が行なっていた[[流鏑馬]]の神事は、その旧臣3人が祭りの日に仙台までやって来て勤めた。[[仙台藩]]は[[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）から彼らに旅費を支給した。後に社職の者も加わって4人が勤めた『源貞氏耳袋』8-58、67頁。。 庶民から「八幡堂」とも呼ばれていたが、古来社名は「大崎八幡'''宮'''」だった[http://rar.miyagi.nii.ac.jp/metadata/703 仙台市文化財調査報告書 大崎八幡宮の松焚祭と裸祭り]（[[仙台市教育委員会]] 2006年12月）。[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）の[[太政官布告・太政官達|太政官布告]]によって[[近代社格制度]]が制定されたのを機に、「大崎八幡'''神社'''」に改称された。鎮座400年の記念大祭を10年後に控えた[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）、「大崎八幡'''宮'''」の名称に復した。 === 年表 === *[[1607年]]（[[慶長]]12年）8月12日 - 社殿造立。 *[[1668年]]（[[寛文]]8年） - 石鳥居を立てた『仙台市の文化財』8頁-9頁。。 *[[1902年]]（[[明治]]35年） - 倒木により石鳥居が損傷した。 *[[1903年]]（明治36年）4月15日 - 大崎八幡神社社殿が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定された文化庁「[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース]」、2011年12月閲覧。『仙台市の文化財』6頁。以下年表中の文化財指定についてはこれら資料による。。 *[[1907年]]（明治40年） - 1902年に壊れた石鳥居を修復した。 *[[1952年]]（昭和27年）11月22日 - 大崎八幡神社社殿（本殿・石の間・拝殿）が[[国宝]]に指定された。 *[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）6月11日 - 大崎八幡神社長床が国の重要文化財に指定された。 *[[1969年]]（昭和44年） - 長床が雨漏りして破損したため、解体修理した。 *[[1970年]]（昭和45年）10月30日 - 大崎八幡神社石鳥居が宮城県指定有形文化財に指定された。 *[[1973年]]（昭和48年）11月5日 - 大崎八幡神社の能神楽が国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]として選択された。 *[[1997年]]（[[平成]]9年）3月 - 木造大元帥明王立像厨子入が仙台市指定有形文化財（彫刻）[http://www.city.sendai.jp/index.html 仙台市ホームページ]、2017年12月17日、閲覧 に指定された。 *[[1997年]]（[[平成]]9年）6月 - 大崎八幡宮に改称した。 *[[2011年]]（平成23年）7月25日 - 大崎八幡宮の旧宮司宿舎、社務所、神馬舎が国の[[登録有形文化財]]に登録された。 == 社殿 == 現存する本殿・石の間・拝殿（国宝）は伊達政宗の寄進により建立され、慶長12年（1607年）に竣工した。手前の拝殿と奥の本殿を「石の間」で繋いで1棟とした、「権現造」形式の社殿である。拝殿は桁行（正面）7間、梁間（側面）3間、入母屋造で、正面には千鳥破風を見せ、唐破風造の向拝が付く（「間」は長さの単位ではなく、「柱間の数」を指す用語。以下も同じ）。本殿は桁行5間、梁間3間、入母屋造。「石の間」は桁行、梁間とも1間、両下造（りょうさげづくり）「切妻造」と似るが、両端が他の建物に接続し、屋根の「妻」を見せないものを指す。 とし、屋根はいずれも杮葺きである。「石の間」は、本殿・拝殿より床を一段低く造り、板敷きとする。本殿の正面軒先部分を「石の間」内部に現すのが、この種「権現造」の特徴的な手法である。拝殿内部には狩野派の絵師佐久間左京の筆に成る唐獅子の障壁画や大虹梁の青龍、石の間の格天井には五十三種の草花が描かれており、俗に左甚五郎の作と伝わる花鳥動植物や説話風の人物、にらみ猫など多彩な彫刻が組み込まれている。本建物は、柱などの軸部を黒漆塗とし、長押、組物、蟇股などは彩色、彫刻、鍍金金具で装飾され、安土桃山時代の文化を今に伝える我国最古の建造物である。『週刊朝日百科日本の国宝』97（朝日新聞社、1998）、pp.'''10''' - 202 - '''10''' - 203（解説筆者は武藤正幸） 桃山建築の粋を凝らした絢爛たる社殿は、のちに日光東照宮に引き継がれることとなる。 境内には樹齢350年の[[イヌシデ]]が立っていたが、環境悪化により枯損した[http://www.city.sendai.jp/midori/__icsFiles/afieldfile/2015/01/22/69.3.pdf 保存樹木の解除について] - [[仙台市]]。 == 主要祭事・年中行事 == * [[1月14日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/matsutaki/index.html 松焚祭]（[[どんと祭]]） * [[2月3日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/setsubun/index.html 節分祭] * [[5月25日]] - [[5月28日|28日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/satsuki/index.html 皐月祭] * [[6月30日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/minazuki/index.html 水無月大祓式] * [[8月12日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/gagaku/index.html 御鎮座記念祭] * [[9月14日]] - [[9月17日|17日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/reitai/index.html 例大祭]（流鏑馬神事も奉納される） ** [[9月1日]]:鳥居祭 * [[12月]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/minazuki/index.html 大祓式] 毎年、[[遠刈田温泉]]の[[刈田嶺神社 (蔵王町遠刈田温泉)|刈田嶺神社（里宮）]]の[[神体]]が春になると[[蔵王連峰|蔵王]]・[[御釜 (蔵王連峰)|御釜]]を見下ろす刈田岳山頂の[[刈田嶺神社 (七ヶ宿町)|刈田嶺神社（奥宮）]]へ季節[[遷座]]しているが、遷座の前に毎年「刈田嶺神社[[雪かき]]奉仕」を当宮が行っている（[[刈田嶺神社 (蔵王町遠刈田温泉)#季節遷座]]参照）[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/thisweek/index.html ■「刈田嶺神社雪かき奉仕」（4月21日）]（大崎八幡宮「八幡さま日記」）。 == 文化財 == [[File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg|thumb|250px|社殿。手前が拝殿、奥の屋根が本殿で、これらの間を「石の間」で繋ぐ。]] '''国宝''' * 本殿、石の間、拝殿（1棟） - 慶長12年（1607年）竣工。[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）国宝指定。解説は既出（「社殿」の項を参照）。 '''重要文化財''' * 長床（1棟） '''登録有形文化財''' *社務所 *旧宮司宿舎 *神馬舎 '''選択無形民俗文化財''' * 能神楽 Ōsaki Hachiman-gū nagatoko.jpg|長床 Ōsaki Hachiman-gū residential house for chief priest.jpg|旧宮司宿舎 Ōsaki Hachiman-gū shinme-sha.jpg|神馬舎 Ōsaki Hachiman-gū ishi-torii.jpg|石鳥居（宮城県指定有形文化財） == 交通 == [[ファイル:Sendai City Bus S6569.jpg|サムネイル|どんと祭臨時バス（2023年1月14日）]] どんと祭の日は、[[パークアンドライド]]用の駐車場が開設されたり、[[仙台市都心部]]との間などに臨時バスが運行されたりする。[[1941年]]（昭和16年）から[[1976年]]（昭和51年）までは[[仙台市電]]・[[仙台市電#八幡町線|八幡神社前駅]]が最寄り駅だった。 *バス **[[ファイル:Miyagi Kotsu S3811.jpg|サムネイル|どんと祭臨時シャトルバス（2023年1月14日）]][[仙台駅のバス乗り場#仙台駅西口バスターミナル|仙台駅西口バスプール]]の10番乗り場（[[西方寺 (仙台市)|定義]]、[[作並温泉]]、白沢車庫、[[芋沢]]、[[大沢村 (宮城県)|大沢]]・青野木、[[茂庭台団地|茂庭]]、西花苑）、または、15番乗り場（[[吉成 (仙台市)|南吉成]]団地、国見ヶ丘）より、[[仙台市営バス]][840]～[888]系統に乗車し、「大崎八幡宮前バス停」下車。 ** [[仙台市地下鉄南北線]]・[[北四番丁駅]]付近の「二日町北四番丁バス停」より、上記系統に乗車し、「大崎八幡宮前バス停」下車。 ** 仙台駅西口バスプールの10番乗り場より、仙台市営バス[830][831][839]系統に乗車し、「大崎八幡宮前バス停」下車。 ** 仙台駅西口バスプールの16番乗り場より、仙台市営バス[[るーぷる仙台]]に乗車し、「大崎八幡宮前バス停」下車。ただし、[[瑞鳳殿]]、[[仙台城]]址、[[青葉山 (宮城県)|青葉山]]、[[宮城県美術館]]を経由するため、これらの施設を巡った後に立ち寄るのには便利だが、仙台駅前から直接訪れるためには、上記の路線バスの約2倍の所要時間（43分）がかかることに注意。 *鉄道 ** [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[仙山線]] *** [[東北福祉大前駅]]から北参道まで[[徒歩所要時間|徒歩]]12分。 *** [[国見駅 (宮城県)|国見駅]]から表参道まで徒歩15分。 *無料[[駐車場]] ** 北参道前にある。普通車：約70台、タクシー：4台、バス：2台 ** 駐車場の出入口は、市道宮脇通線仙台市道青葉672号・宮脇通線、市道小石沢通線仙台市道青葉671号・小石沢通線、市道鈴虫荘公園前線仙台市道青葉677号・鈴虫荘公園前線 がつくる変則五叉路に坂道で接続し、実質的に変則六叉路を形成しているので出入りに注意。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === *作者不詳、「源貞氏耳袋」刊行会・編、吉田正志・監修、『[[源貞氏耳袋]]』8、[[2007年]]。 *仙台市教育委員会・編集発行『仙台市の文化財』、1996年。 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [[宮城県の観光地]] * [[八幡神]] * [[左義長]] * [[流鏑馬]] * [[すずめ踊り]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ 大崎八幡宮] *文化庁「[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース]」。 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{architecture-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおさきはちまんくう}} [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の歴史]] [[Category:宮城県の国宝]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宮城県の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:仙台伊達家|社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:宮城県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū

"plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]][[Category:Hachiman Beppyo shrines to move in template]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]][[Category:Hachiman Beppyo shrines to move in template]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq\]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{beppyo shrines}}]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}}

revision.comment: Trying to fix the templates
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{beppyo shrines}}]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

revision.comment: /* 主要祭事・年中行事 */
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大崎八幡宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Osakimachimangu.JPG|270px]]
拝殿（国宝） |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]八幡四丁目6番1号 |緯度度 = 38 |緯度分 = 16 |緯度秒 = 21.1 |経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 50 |経度秒 = 42 |ISO = JP-04 |祭神 = [[応神天皇]]、[[仲哀天皇]]、[[神功皇后]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月14日]] - [[9月17日|17日]] |神事 = 松焚祭（[[どんと祭]]） |地図 = Sendai#Japan Miyagi }} {{座標一覧}} '''大崎八幡宮'''（おおさきはちまんぐう）は、[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]八幡にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。社殿（本殿・石の間・拝殿）は[[国宝]]に指定されており、[[どんと祭]]の裸参りで知られる。 == 祭神 == * [[応神天皇]]、[[仲哀天皇]]、[[神功皇后]] * [[江戸時代]]に仙台で盛んとなった卦体神（けたいがみ、[[十二支]]の[[守り本尊]]）信仰に伴い、[[乾]]の守り本尊である[[阿弥陀如来]]とされたことから、現在でも戌亥歳生まれの人からの崇敬を集めている。 == 社名・歴史== [[ファイル:NDL-DC 762376 0208 0023crd-OsakiHachimanJinja.jpg|thumb|240px|right|[[1912年]]（[[明治]]45年）頃の大崎八幡神社]] 創建年代は不明であるが、社伝では[[坂上田村麻呂]]が[[宇佐神宮]]を[[鎮守府 (古代)|鎮守府]][[胆沢城]]（現[[岩手県]][[奥州市]]水沢）に[[勧請]]し'''[[鎮守府八幡宮]]'''と称したことに始まり、[[室町時代]]に入り[[奥州管領]]であった[[大崎氏]]が自領地内（現[[宮城県]][[大崎市]][[田尻町 (宮城県)|田尻町]]）に遷したため、大崎八幡宮と呼ばれるようになったという。 大崎氏改易後の[[慶長]]9年（[[1604年]]）、[[伊達政宗]]が[[仙台城]]（{{Coord|38|15|11.2|N|140|51|23.3|E|region:JP-04|name=仙台城（本丸）}}）から見て北西（[[乾]]）かつ仙台城の[[城下町]]（{{Coord|38|15|36.9|N|140|52|14.5|E|region:JP-04|name=仙台城下町の中心地「芭蕉の辻」}}）北西端の現在地に造営を始めた。当地は[[別当寺]]の[[龍寶寺 (仙台市)|龍寶寺]]（{{Coord|38|16|17.4|N|140|50|45|E|region:JP-04|name=龍宝寺（別当寺）}}）の西隣にあり、城下町の北に連なる北山丘陵（[[七北田丘陵]]）と[[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]とが接するために城下町がある[[河岸段丘]]の西端部にあたり、広瀬川上流の[[愛子盆地]]や[[山形県|山形]]方面に向かう[[作並街道]]（現[[国道48号]]）が超えなくてはいけない最初の難所の鶏沢（{{Coord|38|16|12.2|N|140|50|35.5|E|region:JP-04|name=一部暗渠化された鶏沢に架かる鶏橋}}）[http://www.hirosegawa-net.com/100sen/naka_001.php 鶏橋・鶏沢]（仙台市「広瀬川ホームページ」） の城下町側に接する地である[http://eichi.library.pref.miyagi.jp/ezu/ 古絵地図]（[[宮城県図書館]]）[http://www.udworks.net/katteren/katte_site/hachiman/_adventure/pict/2m1786_1.jpg 仙台城下絵図/天明6～寛政元年(1786～89)]（勝手連 仙臺まちづくり応援団「[http://www.udworks.net/katteren/katte_site/hachiman/index.html ゲート・タウン八幡]」）。[[刑部左衛門国次]]([[左甚五郎]]のモデルの一人と言われる)という[[彫刻]]職人が建立に携わった。 [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）、従来[[伊達氏]]が祀ってきた[[成島八幡神社|成島八幡宮]]と合祀して[[遷座]]し、仙台城下の乾（戌亥・北西）[[天門 (方位)|天門]]の鎮めとした（当宮の西隣の鶏沢には堤も造られた）。以前に大崎氏の家臣が行なっていた[[流鏑馬]]の神事は、その旧臣3人が祭りの日に仙台までやって来て勤めた。[[仙台藩]]は[[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）から彼らに旅費を支給した。後に社職の者も加わって4人が勤めた『源貞氏耳袋』8-58、67頁。。 庶民から「八幡堂」とも呼ばれていたが、古来社名は「大崎八幡'''宮'''」だった[http://rar.miyagi.nii.ac.jp/metadata/703 仙台市文化財調査報告書 大崎八幡宮の松焚祭と裸祭り]（[[仙台市教育委員会]] 2006年12月）。[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）の[[太政官布告・太政官達|太政官布告]]によって[[近代社格制度]]が制定されたのを機に、「大崎八幡'''神社'''」に改称された。鎮座400年の記念大祭を10年後に控えた[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）、「大崎八幡'''宮'''」の名称に復した。 === 年表 === *[[1607年]]（[[慶長]]12年）8月12日 - 社殿造立。 *[[1668年]]（[[寛文]]8年） - 石鳥居を立てた『仙台市の文化財』8頁-9頁。。 *[[1902年]]（[[明治]]35年） - 倒木により石鳥居が損傷した。 *[[1903年]]（明治36年）4月15日 - 大崎八幡神社社殿が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定された文化庁「[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース]」、2011年12月閲覧。『仙台市の文化財』6頁。以下年表中の文化財指定についてはこれら資料による。。 *[[1907年]]（明治40年） - 1902年に壊れた石鳥居を修復した。 *[[1952年]]（昭和27年）11月22日 - 大崎八幡神社社殿（本殿・石の間・拝殿）が[[国宝]]に指定された。 *[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）6月11日 - 大崎八幡神社長床が国の重要文化財に指定された。 *[[1969年]]（昭和44年） - 長床が雨漏りして破損したため、解体修理した。 *[[1970年]]（昭和45年）10月30日 - 大崎八幡神社石鳥居が宮城県指定有形文化財に指定された。 *[[1973年]]（昭和48年）11月5日 - 大崎八幡神社の能神楽が国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]として選択された。 *[[1997年]]（[[平成]]9年）3月 - 木造大元帥明王立像厨子入が仙台市指定有形文化財（彫刻）[http://www.city.sendai.jp/index.html 仙台市ホームページ]、2017年12月17日、閲覧 に指定された。 *[[1997年]]（[[平成]]9年）6月 - 大崎八幡宮に改称した。 *[[2011年]]（平成23年）7月25日 - 大崎八幡宮の旧宮司宿舎、社務所、神馬舎が国の[[登録有形文化財]]に登録された。 == 社殿 == 現存する本殿・石の間・拝殿（国宝）は伊達政宗の寄進により建立され、慶長12年（1607年）に竣工した。手前の拝殿と奥の本殿を「石の間」で繋いで1棟とした、「権現造」形式の社殿である。拝殿は桁行（正面）7間、梁間（側面）3間、入母屋造で、正面には千鳥破風を見せ、唐破風造の向拝が付く（「間」は長さの単位ではなく、「柱間の数」を指す用語。以下も同じ）。本殿は桁行5間、梁間3間、入母屋造。「石の間」は桁行、梁間とも1間、両下造（りょうさげづくり）「切妻造」と似るが、両端が他の建物に接続し、屋根の「妻」を見せないものを指す。 とし、屋根はいずれも杮葺きである。「石の間」は、本殿・拝殿より床を一段低く造り、板敷きとする。本殿の正面軒先部分を「石の間」内部に現すのが、この種「権現造」の特徴的な手法である。拝殿内部には狩野派の絵師佐久間左京の筆に成る唐獅子の障壁画や大虹梁の青龍、石の間の格天井には五十三種の草花が描かれており、俗に左甚五郎の作と伝わる花鳥動植物や説話風の人物、にらみ猫など多彩な彫刻が組み込まれている。本建物は、柱などの軸部を黒漆塗とし、長押、組物、蟇股などは彩色、彫刻、鍍金金具で装飾され、安土桃山時代の文化を今に伝える我国最古の建造物である。『週刊朝日百科日本の国宝』97（朝日新聞社、1998）、pp.'''10''' - 202 - '''10''' - 203（解説筆者は武藤正幸） 桃山建築の粋を凝らした絢爛たる社殿は、のちに日光東照宮に引き継がれることとなる。 境内には樹齢350年の[[イヌシデ]]が立っていたが、環境悪化により枯損した[http://www.city.sendai.jp/midori/__icsFiles/afieldfile/2015/01/22/69.3.pdf 保存樹木の解除について] - [[仙台市]]。 == 主要祭事・年中行事 == * [[1月14日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/matsutaki/index.html 松焚祭]（[[どんと祭]]、[[仙台裸参り]]とも呼ばれる） * [[2月3日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/setsubun/index.html 節分祭] * [[5月25日]] - [[5月28日|28日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/satsuki/index.html 皐月祭] * [[6月30日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/minazuki/index.html 水無月大祓式] * [[8月12日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/gagaku/index.html 御鎮座記念祭] * [[9月14日]] - [[9月17日|17日]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/reitai/index.html 例大祭]（流鏑馬神事も奉納される） ** [[9月1日]]:鳥居祭 * [[12月]]:[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/festival/minazuki/index.html 大祓式] 毎年、[[遠刈田温泉]]の[[刈田嶺神社 (蔵王町遠刈田温泉)|刈田嶺神社（里宮）]]の[[神体]]が春になると[[蔵王連峰|蔵王]]・[[御釜 (蔵王連峰)|御釜]]を見下ろす刈田岳山頂の[[刈田嶺神社 (七ヶ宿町)|刈田嶺神社（奥宮）]]へ季節[[遷座]]しているが、遷座の前に毎年「刈田嶺神社[[雪かき]]奉仕」を当宮が行っている（[[刈田嶺神社 (蔵王町遠刈田温泉)#季節遷座]]参照）[http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/thisweek/index.html ■「刈田嶺神社雪かき奉仕」（4月21日）]（大崎八幡宮「八幡さま日記」）。 == 文化財 == [[File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg|thumb|250px|社殿。手前が拝殿、奥の屋根が本殿で、これらの間を「石の間」で繋ぐ。]] '''国宝''' * 本殿、石の間、拝殿（1棟） - 慶長12年（1607年）竣工。[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）国宝指定。解説は既出（「社殿」の項を参照）。 '''重要文化財''' * 長床（1棟） '''登録有形文化財''' *社務所 *旧宮司宿舎 *神馬舎 '''選択無形民俗文化財''' * 能神楽 Ōsaki Hachiman-gū nagatoko.jpg|長床 Ōsaki Hachiman-gū residential house for chief priest.jpg|旧宮司宿舎 Ōsaki Hachiman-gū shinme-sha.jpg|神馬舎 Ōsaki Hachiman-gū ishi-torii.jpg|石鳥居（宮城県指定有形文化財） == 交通 == [[ファイル:Sendai City Bus S6569.jpg|サムネイル|どんと祭臨時バス（2023年1月14日）]] どんと祭の日は、[[パークアンドライド]]用の駐車場が開設されたり、[[仙台市都心部]]との間などに臨時バスが運行されたりする。[[1941年]]（昭和16年）から[[1976年]]（昭和51年）までは[[仙台市電]]・[[仙台市電#八幡町線|八幡神社前駅]]が最寄り駅だった。 *バス **[[ファイル:Miyagi Kotsu S3811.jpg|サムネイル|どんと祭臨時シャトルバス（2023年1月14日）]][[仙台駅のバス乗り場#仙台駅西口バスターミナル|仙台駅西口バスプール]]の10番乗り場（[[西方寺 (仙台市)|定義]]、[[作並温泉]]、白沢車庫、[[芋沢]]、[[大沢村 (宮城県)|大沢]]・青野木、[[茂庭台団地|茂庭]]、西花苑）、または、15番乗り場（[[吉成 (仙台市)|南吉成]]団地、国見ヶ丘）より、[[仙台市営バス]][840]～[888]系統に乗車し、「大崎八幡宮前バス停」下車。 ** [[仙台市地下鉄南北線]]・[[北四番丁駅]]付近の「二日町北四番丁バス停」より、上記系統に乗車し、「大崎八幡宮前バス停」下車。 ** 仙台駅西口バスプールの10番乗り場より、仙台市営バス[830][831][839]系統に乗車し、「大崎八幡宮前バス停」下車。 ** 仙台駅西口バスプールの16番乗り場より、仙台市営バス[[るーぷる仙台]]に乗車し、「大崎八幡宮前バス停」下車。ただし、[[瑞鳳殿]]、[[仙台城]]址、[[青葉山 (宮城県)|青葉山]]、[[宮城県美術館]]を経由するため、これらの施設を巡った後に立ち寄るのには便利だが、仙台駅前から直接訪れるためには、上記の路線バスの約2倍の所要時間（43分）がかかることに注意。 *鉄道 ** [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[仙山線]] *** [[東北福祉大前駅]]から北参道まで[[徒歩所要時間|徒歩]]12分。 *** [[国見駅 (宮城県)|国見駅]]から表参道まで徒歩15分。 *無料[[駐車場]] ** 北参道前にある。普通車：約70台、タクシー：4台、バス：2台 ** 駐車場の出入口は、市道宮脇通線仙台市道青葉672号・宮脇通線、市道小石沢通線仙台市道青葉671号・小石沢通線、市道鈴虫荘公園前線仙台市道青葉677号・鈴虫荘公園前線 がつくる変則五叉路に坂道で接続し、実質的に変則六叉路を形成しているので出入りに注意。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === *作者不詳、「源貞氏耳袋」刊行会・編、吉田正志・監修、『[[源貞氏耳袋]]』8、[[2007年]]。 *仙台市教育委員会・編集発行『仙台市の文化財』、1996年。 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [[宮城県の観光地]] * [[八幡神]] * [[左義長]] * [[流鏑馬]] * [[すずめ踊り]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ 大崎八幡宮] *文化庁「[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース]」。 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{architecture-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおさきはちまんくう}}
