[[Category:宮城県の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:仙台伊達家|社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:宮城県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]]\[[Category:Completion tier 2]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū

The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:Pages of u]][[Category:ginality]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 1]]\[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq\]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:Pages of u]][[Category:ginality]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 1]]\[[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). {{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11\]] [[Category:ginality]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[ja:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}}

All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11\]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[ja:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11\]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[ja:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}}

"plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[ja:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[ja:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}}

The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[ja:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}}

"plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[ja:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[Category:Ja import failed]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[ja:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[sn: failed]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[ja:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:大崎八幡宮]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Osaki Hachimangu}} [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:大崎八幡宮]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]].
