"plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:C]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[Category:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]]

大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]] [[Category:仙台伊達家]]

All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]] [[Category:仙台伊達家]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の歴史]] [[Category:宮城県の国宝]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]]

The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]] [[Category:仙台伊達家]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の歴史]] [[Category:宮城県の国宝]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:宮城県の登録有形文化財]]

"plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]] [[Category:仙台伊達家]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の歴史]] [[Category:宮城県の国宝]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:宮城県の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宮城県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]] [[Category:仙台伊達家]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の歴史]] [[Category:宮城県の国宝]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:宮城県の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宮城県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]]

"plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]] [[Category:仙台伊達家]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の歴史]] [[Category:宮城県の国宝]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:宮城県の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宮城県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]] [[Category:仙台伊達家]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市青葉区の歴史]] [[Category:宮城県の国宝]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:宮城県の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宮城県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). {{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). {{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' [[Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[:en:Gabled roof|Gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[:en:Bargeboard|Bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). {{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[:en:Gabled roof|Gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[dormer]] with a decorative [[:en:Bargeboard|Bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[:en:Irimoya|Irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[:en:Gabled roof|Gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[:en:Dormer|Dormer]] with a decorative [[:en:Bargeboard|Bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]] [[Category:Date family of Sendai]] [[Category:Buildings in Aoba-ku, Sendai]] All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[Ken (architecture)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[:en:Irimoya|Irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[:en:Gabled roof|Gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[:en:Dormer|Dormer]] with a decorative [[:en:Bargeboard|Bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[:en:Karahafu|Karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). {{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[:en:Ken (unit)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[:en:Irimoya|Irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[:en:Gabled roof|Gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[:en:Dormer|Dormer]] with a decorative [[:en:Bargeboard|Bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[:en:Karahafu|Karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[National Treasures of Japan|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[Meiji period]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[:en:Ken (unit)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[:en:Irimoya|Irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[:en:Gabled roof|Gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[:en:Dormer|Dormer]] with a decorative [[:en:Bargeboard|Bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[:en:Karahafu|Karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大崎八幡宮|version=103848578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Date clan]] [[Category:National Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]] [[Category:Date family of Sendai]] [[Category:Buildings in Aoba-ku, Sendai]] [[Category:History of Aoba Ward, Sendai]] [[Category:宮城県の国宝]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Miyagi prefecture]] All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[:en:Ken (unit)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[:en:Irimoya|Irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[:en:Gabled roof|Gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[:en:Dormer|Dormer]] with a decorative [[:en:Bargeboard|Bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[:en:Karahafu|Karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). {{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[Sendai]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[:en:Ken (unit)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[:en:Irimoya|Irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[:en:Gabled roof|Gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[:en:Dormer|Dormer]] with a decorative [[:en:Bargeboard|Bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[:en:Karahafu|Karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Ōsaki Hachimangū
大崎八幡宮 | image = File:Osaki Hachiman-gu 20130819b.jpg | caption = ''Haiden'' of Ōsaki Hachimangū | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|38.2722|N|140.845|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman]] | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 4-6-1 Hachiman, [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]] | website = {{URL|http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōsaki Hachimangū'''|大崎八幡宮}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]], [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The {{nihongo|main shrine building|社殿|shaden}} has been designated a [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure of Japan]]. ==History== The construction of the present ''shaden'' was ordered by the wealthy ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]]. He hired craftsmen from [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] and central Japan, some of whom had previously worked for the [[Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi family.]] The shrine was erected between 1604 and 1607. The lavish decoration with wood carving, painting, metal fittings, and lacquer emulated recent models from central Japan, in particular, the [[Toyokuni Shrine (Kyoto)|Toyokuni shrine]] in Kyoto.{{Cite book|title=Osaki Hachiman: Architecture, Materiality and Samurai Power in Seventeenth-Century Japan|last=Schweizer|first=Anton|publisher=Dietrich Reimer Verlag GmbH|year=2016|isbn=978-3496015413|location=Berlin|pages=206–228}} Since the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] the shrine was called Ōsaki Hachiman Jinja. In consideration of historical circumstances, its original name was reinstated in June 1997. On November 22, 1952, the ''shaden'' was designated as a National Treasure. ==Architecture== The ''shaden'' is one of the oldest extant examples of {{nihongo|''[[ishi-no-ma-zukuri]]''|石の間造}}, also known as ''[[Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]]'' (権現造), and an outstanding work of [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama]] architecture. It is a single-storied structure consisting of a main sanctuary (''[[honden]]'') and a worship hall (''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'') which are joined via a connecting passage called ''ishi-no-ma''. All three structures are under a single roof which is covered with thin [[:en:Shake (shingle)|shake shingles]] (''kokerabuki'' 柿葺) made from Japanese cypress.{{cite web | url = http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf | title = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine – English information | publisher = Ōsaki Hachiman Shrine | access-date = 2009-11-04 }}{{Cite web | publisher = [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] | script-title = ja:国指定文化財 データベース | trans-title = Database of National Cultural Properties | access-date = 2009-04-16 | date = 2008-11-01 | url = http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | language = ja | archive-date = 2009-03-30 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20090330140022/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp | url-status = dead }} The ''honden'' is a 5 [[:en:Ken (unit)|ken]] (間, bay) by 3 ken structure with a hip-and-gable, [[:en:Irimoya|Irimoya]] style roof to which a simple [[:en:Gabled roof|Gabled roof]] of the 3 ken by 1 ken ''ishi-no-ma'' connects. The ''haiden'' is also 3 ken deep and 7 ken wide. Its roof is like that of the ''honden'' of the hip-and-gable type. On the front it has an attached triangular [[:en:Dormer|Dormer]] with a decorative [[:en:Bargeboard|Bargeboard]] of strongly concave shape, a {{nihongo|''chidori hafu''|千鳥破風|lit. "plover gable"}}.{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/c/chidorihafu.htm | title = chidori hafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} The entrance is covered by a 5 ken wide step canopy with an undulating ''[[:en:Karahafu|Karahafu]]'' gable at eave ends{{cite web | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/karahafu.htm | title = karahafu | publisher = [[JAANUS|JAANUS – Japanese Architecture and Art Net User System]] | access-date = 2009-11-08 }} (''nokikarahafu''). ==See also== *[[:en:List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)|List of National Treasures of Japan (shrines)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category|Osaki-Hachiman-gu}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/ Ōsaki Hachimangū official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [http://www.okos.co.jp/oosaki/img/english.pdf Ōsaki Hachimangū English pamphlet] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Hachiman Faith}} 2]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]][[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]]}} [[sn:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[en:Ōsaki Hachimangū]] [[sn:saki Hachimangū]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:大崎八幡宮]] [[Category:Date family of Sendai]]
